extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. Reasoning: (1) sent is set true ONLY by t2 (send_attempt), whose guard is `not opted_out`, so sent can become true only while opted_out is false. (2) opted_out becomes true ONLY via t1 (optout), which atomically also sets sent := false; once true it never reverts (no transition sets opted_out false). (3) While opted_out is true: t2 is disabled (guard `not opted_out` fails), and the only enabled transitions touching sent are t1 (sent:=false), t3 (send_attempt when opted_out -> sent:=false), and t4 (tick -> sent:=false); an event with no matching transition leaves state unchanged. Hence once opted_out turns true, sent is false at that instant and no transition can ever set it true again. The predicate (opted_out implies not sent) holds initially (opted_out=false, vacuously) and is preserved by every transition, making it an inductive invariant. Exhaustive BFS over the full reachable state space (18 reachable states, ticks bounded 0-5) found zero states with opted_out=true and sent=true, well within the 9-event bound. Therefore the property holds and no counterexample exists.
